items.text: ScheduleId newtype and Schedule configuration type exist in aion-core with cron expression, interval, overlap policy, and catch-up policy.
Schedule event family exists: ScheduleCreated, ScheduleUpdated, SchedulePaused, ScheduleResumed, ScheduleDeleted, ScheduleTriggered.
OverlapPolicy enum with Skip, BufferOne, CancelPrevious, AllowAll variants.
CatchUpPolicy enum with All, One, Skip variants.
ScheduleEvaluator uses durable timers (AT infrastructure) to fire triggers at the correct time without polling.
Overlap policy is enforced: Skip does not start a new workflow when the previous is still running.
Catch-up policy is enforced on recovery: missed triggers are handled according to policy.
Engine API: create_schedule, update_schedule, pause_schedule, resume_schedule, delete_schedule, list_schedules, describe_schedule.
aion-server exposes schedule management via gRPC and HTTP endpoints.
Recovery reconstructs schedule state from events and re-arms timers for all active schedules.
Cron expression parsing uses a well-tested crate and supports standard 5-field expressions.
Integration test: a schedule fires on interval, overlap policy prevents concurrent executions, recovery re-arms after restart.
